title: salix × quercifolia
intro: Salix × quercifolia, also known as the willow-leaved oak willow, is a hybrid plant species derived from the cross between the Salix alba (white willow) and the Quercus robur (English oak). The bark and leaves of the Salix × quercifolia contain salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making it a potential natural remedy for various health issues. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and soothe digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Salix × quercifolia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
